title: rubus macrophyllus subsp. belophorus
intro: Rubus macrophyllus subsp. belophorus, also known as the Japanese black raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus macrophyllus plant, which is native to East Asia and belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The leaves and stems of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Rubus macrophyllus subsp. belophorus are numerous, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as possessing antimicrobial and antitumor activities. Rubus macrophyllus subsp. belophorus can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
